args: I like long-duration growth equities here (names like high-multiple software and unprofitable-but-scaling tech) because the rate-cutting cycle is starting. Lower discount rates disproportionately re-rate long-duration cash flows, and growth has historically outperformed value in the first 6-12 months after the first cut in a cycle. The Fed's own dot plot points to at least two more cuts this year, and inflation has been trending down for three straight quarters, so the path of least resistance for rates is down. I'm not picking individual names -- this is a sector/style rotation call: overweight long-duration growth, underweight short-duration value and cash-flow-heavy defensives, funded out of the money-market cash I've been sitting in since last year.
note: Pass-1-only, no tickers -> Position-Level Read omitted; exercises macro/theme/implicit layers plus an opportunity-cost (cash-funding) assumption and a distinct dot-plot 'two more cuts' claim

args: I want to go long PLTR.O: the software business keeps compounding, margins expand as the platform scales, and the AI-analytics moat is durable. The invest-now-harvest-later story implicitly assumes the company can keep funding expansion cheaply.
note: Single name + implicit financing-cost assumption; tests layer-4 extraction + Phase 3 position read

args: AI infrastructure -- semiconductors, data-center hardware, power/cooling suppliers -- is the best risk-adjusted way to play the AI buildout over 2-3 years, because the infrastructure layer captures hyperscaler capex regardless of which application-layer company wins.
note: Sector/theme thesis, fragile hyperscaler-capex demand assumption; no tickers -> Phase 3 skipped

args: I want to buy TSLA.O because it's been going up and everyone I follow is bullish, Elon is a genius, the stock always comes back, and robotaxis are coming soon. I think it could double.
note: Deliberately weak -- confirmation-bias check: skill must push back, not rubber-stamp
